data_description.device_profile: Select the appropriate device profile for your Zendure system. The profile contains tuned control parameters.
data_description.soc_entity: Sensor with the current battery state of charge in percent. Central basis for all decisions.
data_description.soc_limit_entity: Optional sensor from the Zendure integration that reports active BMS limits, such as charge or discharge blocking.
data_description.pack_capacity_kwh: Usable capacity of a single battery pack in kWh. This is multiplied by the number of installed packs to calculate the total capacity.
data_description.battery_ac_power_entity: Sensor for the AC power of the battery. The value must be positive during discharge. Recommended: sensor.solarflow_2400_ac_bat_in_out
data_description.additional_battery_charge_entity: Optional sensor of a second battery system. If this sensor reports positive charging power, Battery SmartFlow AI blocks discharge.
data_description.capacity_entity: Optional sensor with the automatically calculated available battery capacity in kWh, for example from the Zendure integration. If set, it overrides the manual calculation.
data_description.installed_pv_wp: Installed theoretical PV module power of the system in Wp. This value is used for relative summer/winter thresholds and further PV-related logic.
data_description.pv_entity: Sensor with the current PV power in watts. Used to detect surplus.
data_description.price_now_entity: Sensor with the current electricity price, for example from Tibber. Used for economic decisions.
data_description.price_export_entity: Sensor with price forecast data, for example Tibber / EPEX. Enables price-based planning.
data_description.grid_mode: Choose how your grid consumption is measured: one combined sensor or separate sensors for import and export.
data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power during grid import and negative power during export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power, positive values only.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power, positive values only.
data_description.ac_mode_entity: Select entity from the Zendure integration that switches between charging (INPUT) and discharging (OUTPUT).
data_description.input_limit_entity: Number entity used to set charging power in watts.
data_description.output_limit_entity: Number entity used to set discharging power in watts.

reconfigure_grid.title: Configure grid measurement
reconfigure_grid.description: Select the grid sensors matching the mode

data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power during import and negative power during export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power in watts.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power in watts.

grid.title: Configure grid measurement
grid.description: Select the grid sensors matching the mode

data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power during import and negative power during export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power in watts.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power in watts.

data_description.device_profile: Select the appropriate device profile for your Zendure system. Control parameters are adjusted automatically.
data_description.soc_entity: Sensor with the current battery state of charge in percent.
data_description.soc_limit_entity: Optional status sensor from the Zendure integration to detect active BMS limits.
data_description.pack_capacity_kwh: Usable capacity of a single battery pack in kWh. This is multiplied by the number of installed packs to calculate the total capacity.
data_description.battery_ac_power_entity: Sensor for the AC power of the battery. The value must be positive during discharge. Recommended: sensor.solarflow_2400_ac_bat_in_out
data_description.additional_battery_charge_entity: Optional sensor of a second battery system. If this sensor reports positive charging power, Battery SmartFlow AI blocks discharge.
data_description.capacity_entity: Optional sensor with the automatically calculated available battery capacity in kWh, for example from the Zendure integration. If set, it overrides the manual calculation.
data_description.installed_pv_wp: Installed theoretical PV module power of the system in Wp. This value is used for relative summer/winter thresholds and further PV-related logic.
data_description.pv_entity: Sensor with the current PV power in watts.
data_description.price_now_entity: Current electricity price. Enables price-based discharge.
data_description.price_export_entity: Price forecast data for the next hours. Enables intelligent charging planning.
data_description.grid_mode: Defines how grid import and export are measured.
data_description.grid_power_entity: A combined grid sensor (positive = import, negative = export).
data_description.ac_mode_entity: Zendure AC mode (INPUT/OUTPUT). Controlled automatically by the integration.

init.title: Edit control profile
init.description: Expert settings for the active device profile. These values change the internal control behavior and should only be adjusted if you understand their effect.

data_description.TARGET_IMPORT_W: Target value for a small intentional grid import. A higher value reduces the risk of unwanted export but slightly increases grid consumption.
data_description.DEADBAND_W: Tolerance range in which no control action is taken. A higher value makes control calmer, a lower value more aggressive and precise.
data_description.EXPORT_GUARD_W: Additional safety margin against unwanted grid export. Higher values make control more cautious.
data_description.KP_UP: Gain used when increasing power. Higher values react faster but may overshoot more easily.
data_description.KP_DOWN: Gain used when decreasing power. Higher values reduce power faster but may behave less smoothly.
data_description.MAX_STEP_UP: Maximum power step per control cycle when increasing power. Higher values make control faster but potentially more aggressive.
data_description.MAX_STEP_DOWN: Maximum power step per control cycle when decreasing power. Higher values reduce faster but may cause stronger jumps.
data_description.KEEPALIVE_MIN_DEFICIT_W: Minimum deficit required to keep discharge active. Helps prevent oscillation at very small loads.
data_description.KEEPALIVE_MIN_OUTPUT_W: Minimum discharge power to keep discharge stably active. Prevents unnecessary switching on and off.

state.expensive_discharge: Discharging due to high electricity price
state.very_expensive_force_discharge: Forced discharge due to extreme price spike
